---
title: "Week 9 Milestone Tables"
author: "<img src='images/logo.png' width='50' /> <br> Dairy Cow Foot Doc"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    theme: flatly
    df_print: paged
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: false
    code-fold: true
execute:
  message: false
  warning: false
  echo: false
embed-resources: true
params:
  event:
---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  broom,
  tidyverse,
  dtplyr,
  gt,
  arrow,
  rmarkdown,
  lubridate,
  quarto, 
  arrow,
  survival,
  survminer,
  ggsurvfit,
  flextable,
  gtsummary,
  marginaleffects,
  scales,
  zoo
  )

events_formatted <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/events_formatted.parquet') 

animals <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/animals.parquet')

deno_cal <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/denominator_by_calendar_time_period.parquet') |> 
  filter(deno_type == "lact_basic" & 
           calendar_time_period_type == "year") |> 
  select(-c(contains("dop"), day_of_phase_group))

  
# load GC's functions
source(file.path('functions/fxn_load_os_fxns.R'))
fxn_load_os_fxns()

# Load Parnell CR fucntions from Github
cr_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ParnellPharma/ParnellFunctionsPublic/main/parnell_calculate_CR_master.R"
source(cr_url)

# create variables for inline code
farm_name <- unique(events_formatted$location_event)

date_max_pull <- max(events_formatted$date_event, na.rm = TRUE)
date_min_pull <- date_max_pull - years(1)

# pull solution data


```

```{r}
#| label: functions to hide my code
#| echo: true

solutions_deno <- function () {
  
deno_table <- deno_cal |> 
  select(location_lact_list, 
         date_time_period_start,
         group = 'Lactation Group' , ct_animal_days_elig, 
         ct_animal_time_periods, ct_animal_lactations,
         ct_animals) |>
  mutate(group = if_else(group == "LACT > 0",
                                    "Adult Cows", group)
         ) |>
  gt(groupname_col = "location_lact_list",
     rowname_col = "date_time_period_start") |> 
  fmt_date(columns = date_time_period_start,
           date_style = "year") |> 
  fmt_number(columns = c(starts_with("ct")), decimals = 0) |> 
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) |>
    cols_label(
      group = "Group",
    date_time_period_start = "Time Period",
    ct_animal_days_elig = "Days at risk",
    ct_animal_time_periods = "DAR Animals",
    ct_animal_lactations = "Lactations",
    ct_animals = "Animals"
    ) |> 
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Count of",
    columns = c(ct_animal_days_elig, ct_animal_time_periods,
                ct_animal_lactations, ct_animals)
  ) |>
    tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_spanners()
  ) |> 
    tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) |> 
  tab_options(
    row_group.font.weight = "bold" # Bolds the Location  
  ) 
    
    
deno_table

}

# CR

solutions_cr <- function () {
  
cr_data <- events_formatted |> 
  select(id_animal, date_birth, date_fresh,lact_number, lact_group_basic, 
         date_archived, date_event, event, 
         remark_letters1, protocols_letters1, location_event, R) |> 
  mutate(floor_date_time = floor_date(date_event, "year")) |>
  filter(date_event <= date_max_pull - days(40)) |>
  fxn_create_CR_reports(grp_vars = c("location_event", 
                                     "floor_date_time", 
                                     "lact_group_basic", "Rstandard"))

cr_table <- cr_data |> 
  select(location_event, floor_date_time,
         lact_group_basic, `Not Bred Event`,
         Preg, Total, SPC, CR, CI_lower, CI_upper) |> 
  mutate(CR = CR*100,
         CR_graph = CR,
         lact_group_basic = if_else(lact_group_basic == "LACT > 0",
                                    "Adult Cows", lact_group_basic)) |> 
  arrange(location_event, floor_date_time, lact_group_basic) |>
  # 1. Group by location_event to "merge" that column into group headers
  gt(groupname_col = "location_event",
     rowname_col = "floor_date_time") |> 
  # 2. Bold the column labels
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) |> 
  # Optional: Clean up the date display
  fmt_date(columns = floor_date_time, date_style = "year") |> 
  fmt_number(c(CR, CI_lower, CI_upper, SPC), 
             decimals = 1) |> 
  fmt_number(columns = `Not Bred Event`, decimals = 0) |> 
  tab_options(
    row_group.font.weight = "bold" # Bolds the Location  
  )  |> 
  cols_merge_range(col_begin = CI_lower,
                  col_end = CI_upper) |> 
  cols_label(
    floor_date_time = "Time Period",
    lact_group_basic = "Lactation",
    CR = "Risk",
    CI_lower = "95% CI",
    ) |>
  cols_nanoplot(columns = c(CR_graph),
                plot_type = "bar",
                new_col_name = "Plot",
                new_col_label = md("**Plot**"),
                options = nanoplot_options(data_bar_fill_color = "orange",
                                           data_bar_stroke_color = "black")
    ) |> 
  summary_rows(
    groups = everything(), # Adds summary to every group
    columns = c(CR), # Columns you want to summarize
    fns = list(
      "Location Average" = ~mean(., na.rm = TRUE)
    ),
    fmt = ~fmt_number(., columns = c(CR), decimals = 1)
    )|> 
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Conception (%)",
    columns = c(CR, CI_lower, 'Plot')
  ) |>
    tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_spanners()
  ) |> 
  cols_align(align = "center",
             columns = everything()) |> 
  tab_style(locations = cells_summary(),
             style = list(
               cell_text(weight = "bold"),
               cell_fill("lightblue")
             )
            ) 
 cr_table
}

# mast cr data

solutions_mast  <- function () {
  
  pre_mast <- events_formatted |> 
  filter(event == "MAST") |> 
  select(location_event, id_animal_lact, lact_number, date_event,
         event, protocols, remark, lact_group_basic,
         dim_event) |> 
  mutate(mast_60 = if_else(dim_event <=60, 1, 0)) |>
  fxn_dz_status(id_col = id_animal_lact,
                disease_cols = "mast_60", 
                prefix = "mast",
                gap = 14) |> 
  mutate(status_mast_60 = if_else(is.na(status_mast_60), 
                                  "After", status_mast_60)
  ) |> 
  select(id_animal_lact = id_animal, location_event, lact_number,
         mast_60, life_times_mast_60,
         status_mast_60) |> 
  group_by(location_event, id_animal_lact, lact_number, mast_60) |>
  # get last mast mast_60 just using life time ignores mast_60
  slice_max(order_by = life_times_mast_60, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |> 
  ungroup() |> 
  # now will only end up with 1 row/cow
  group_by(location_event, id_animal_lact, lact_number) |>
  slice_max(order_by = mast_60, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
  ungroup()
 
mast_60 <- events_formatted |> 
  left_join(pre_mast,
            by = c("location_event", "id_animal_lact", "lact_number")
            ) |>
  mutate(
    status_mast_60 = if_else(is.na(status_mast_60), 
                             "No Mast", status_mast_60)
  )


cr_data_mast <- mast_60 |> 
  select(id_animal, date_birth, date_fresh,lact_number, lact_group_basic, 
         date_archived, date_event, event, status_mast_60,
         remark_letters1, protocols_letters1, location_event, R) |> 
  mutate(floor_date_time = floor_date(date_event, "year")) |>
  filter(date_event <= date_max_pull - days(40)) |>
  filter(lact_number > 0) |>
  fxn_create_CR_reports(grp_vars = c("location_event", 
                                     "floor_date_time", 
                                     "lact_group_basic", "status_mast_60",
                                     "Rstandard")
                        )

  mast_table <- cr_data_mast |> 
  filter(floor_date_time >= date_max_pull - years(3)) |>
  select(location_event, floor_date_time, status_mast_60,
         Preg, Total,
         CR, CI_lower, CI_upper) |> 
  mutate(CR = CR*100,
         CR_graph = CR,
         ) |> 
  arrange(location_event, floor_date_time) |>
  # 1. Group by location_event to "merge" that column into group headers
  gt(groupname_col = "location_event",
     rowname_col = "floor_date_time") |> 
  # 2. Bold the column labels
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) |> 
  # Optional: Clean up the date display
  fmt_date(columns = floor_date_time, date_style = "year") |> 
  fmt_number(c(CR, CI_lower, CI_upper), 
             decimals = 1) |> 
  tab_options(
    row_group.font.weight = "bold" # Bolds the Location  
  )  |> 
  cols_merge_range(col_begin = CI_lower,
                  col_end = CI_upper) |> 
  cols_label(
    status_mast_60 = "Mast <= 60 DIM",
    CR = "Risk",
    CI_lower = "95% CI",
    ) |>
  cols_nanoplot(columns = c(CR_graph),
                plot_type = "bar",
                new_col_name = "Plot",
                new_col_label = md("**Plot**"),
                reference_line = 50,
                options = nanoplot_options(data_bar_fill_color = "orange",
                                           data_bar_stroke_color = "black")
    ) |> 
  tab_spanner(
    label = "Conception (%)",
    columns = c(CR, CI_lower, 'Plot')
  ) |>
    tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_column_spanners()
  ) |> 
  cols_align(align = "center",
             columns = everything()) |> 
  tab_footnote(footnote = md("**After** = Mastitis after 60 days in milk <br>
                   **New** = First case  <br>
                   **Repeat** = Multiple cases in gap <br>
                   **Chronic** = At least 1 case after gap <br>
                   **No Mast** = No mastitis cases in lactation"),
              locations = cells_column_labels(columns = status_mast_60 
                                              ),
              placement = "right"
                   
  )

mast_table

}


```

# Report for `r farm_name[[1]]` and `r farm_name[[2]]`

## Objective

The objective of this milestone is start working with multiple herds, create tables, use the denominator data and use external functions. The data and functions you need are loaded in the top chunk. This is in prep for milestone 10 where we will use parameters to run the same report for multiple herds.

The steps for this milestone are as follows (code sections for these steps are below)

1.  Re-Run **step0_master_processing.R**
2.  Describe the herd size for the past 5 years for the 2 herds
3.  Run the parnell CR function, by calling **fxn_create_CR_reports**
    1.  More details about the function can be found [here](https://github.com/ParnellPharma/ParnellFunctionsPublic/blob/main/parnell_calculate_CR_master.R)
4.  Run the **fxn_dz_status** as it relates to mastitis to determine if type early mastitis case influences conception risk.
5.  Create tables from the inventory and both conception risk data frames.

## Describe Herds

-   Using the deno_cal dataframe recreate this table using the gt table skills you learned this week. You will probably need to look at the package website or use AI to get some of the details.

-   Some gt function hints are:

    -   groupname_col and rowname_col inside gt()

    -   fmt_data

    -   fmt_number

    -   tab_style

    -   cols_label

    -   tab_spanner

    -   tab_options

```{r}
#| label: describe-herds

solutions_deno()


```

## Conception Risk Tables

Use the Parnell CR function to compare the conception risks between the 2 herds and recreate this table

1.  The **fxn_create_CR_reports** function will work if you just pipe the events_formatted dataframe to it.
2.  You will need define the grp_vars inside the function

-   the last one should be Rstandard
-   the table uses lact_group_basic as one of the variables to group the data

3.  for the table use some of the same gt function you used in describing the herd sizes.

-   additional functions to explore are:
-   summary_rows
-   cols_nanoplot \_ cols_merge_range

```{r}
#| label: create-cr_table

solutions_cr()

```

## Mastitis and Conception Risk

Now recreate the table low were a variable was created to determine if a cow had mastitis in her first 60 DIM.

1.  Once you create the new mastitis variable then run the **fxn_dz_status**

    -   The gap should be 14 days

    -   If the cow had mastitis in the first 60 DIM then she will be classified as

        -   "New" if it was her first case

        -   "Repeat" if it was a subsequent case within the gap

        -   "Chronic" if she had at least 1 case after the gap

2.  After running this function

    -   assign cows that had mastitis \>60 DIM as "After"

3.  Merge the data back with events formatted.

    -   I have provided some code below to help you get started.

    -   After the join, cows with no mastitis events should be coded as No Mast

4.  After you merged the mastitis status variable back into events_formatted run the **fxn_create_CR_reports** function again to get the conception risk by mastitis status

5.  Then create a table to show the results

```{r}
#| label: mast

solutions_mast()

# code to help you
# run this after you've run fxn_disease_status and before you join back to events_formatted

# your code that runs fxn_dz_status should create this data frame
# select(id_animal_lact = id_animal, location_event, lact_number,
#          mast_60, life_times_mast_60,
#          status_mast_60) |> 
#   group_by(location_event, id_animal_lact, lact_number, mast_60) |>
#   # get last mast mast_60 just using life time ignores mast_60
#   slice_max(order_by = life_times_mast_60, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |> 
#   ungroup() |> 
#   # now will only end up with 1 row/cow
#   group_by(location_event, id_animal_lact, lact_number) |>
#   slice_max(order_by = mast_60, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>
#   ungroup()

# then join back to events_formatted
 



```

# Extension

For this week extension continue to create standard things you would want to report or incorporate modeling techniques to explore the data

Ideas

-   Try the code with other diseases like mastitis or culture information
